edges.mechanism: market adjustment to changed demand conditions
edges.evidence: The data suggests that graphite markets exhibit significant responsiveness to both demand shocks (financial crisis) and supply shocks (export restrictions)

edges.mechanism: market adjustment to constrained supply conditions
edges.evidence: The data suggests that graphite markets exhibit significant responsiveness to both demand shocks (financial crisis) and supply shocks (export restrictions)

edges.to: 2-3 year adjustment periods for new supply capacity
edges.mechanism: time required for capacity expansion and market rebalancing
edges.evidence: with adjustment periods of 2-3 years for new supply capacity

edges.from: China's export quotas reduced by 30-40% compared to 2009 levels
edges.mechanism: direct administrative limitation on the quantity of graphite that could be exported
edges.evidence: Export quotas reduced by 30-40% compared to 2009 levels

edges.from: Licensing requirements tightened for mineral exports
edges.to: reduced access to export markets for graphite producers
edges.mechanism: increased administrative barriers making it more difficult for companies to obtain export permits
edges.evidence: Licensing requirements tightened for mineral exports

edges.from: Environmental compliance standards raised for mining operations
edges.to: increased operational costs for graphite mining companies
edges.mechanism: higher regulatory requirements forcing mining operations to invest in environmental compliance measures
edges.evidence: Environmental compliance standards raised for mining operations

edges.from: supply chain constraints and export quota discussions in China
edges.to: spot price increases of 25-40% above 2007 levels
edges.mechanism: supply disruption in the dominant supplier market creating upward pressure on prices
edges.evidence: Price volatility was observed in Q3 2008, with spot prices increasing 25-40% above 2007 levels due to supply chain constraints and export quota discussions in China.

edges.to: growing dependence on imports from China and other suppliers
edges.mechanism: insufficient domestic supply forces reliance on foreign sources to meet demand
edges.evidence: Limited domestic production capacity... Growing dependence on imports from China and other suppliers

edges.from: strong industrial demand from steel and automotive sectors
edges.to: natural graphite imports to the United States increased significantly
edges.mechanism: increased industrial demand drives up import volumes to meet consumption needs
edges.evidence: Natural graphite imports to the United States increased significantly in 2008... This increase was driven by several factors: 1. Strong industrial demand from steel and automotive sectors

edges.to: natural graphite imports to the United States increased significantly
edges.mechanism: insufficient domestic supply forces increased reliance on imports to meet demand
edges.evidence: Natural graphite imports to the United States increased significantly in 2008... This increase was driven by several factors: ...2. Limited domestic production capacity

edges.from: growing dependence on imports from China and other suppliers
edges.to: natural graphite imports to the United States increased significantly
edges.mechanism: increased reliance on foreign suppliers leads to higher import volumes
edges.evidence: Natural graphite imports to the United States increased significantly in 2008... This increase was driven by several factors: ...3. Growing dependence on imports from China and other suppliers
